verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface during epididymal transit, becoming stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane (postacrosomal/equatorial segment) in a wash-resistant manner via tight non-covalent or covalent association acquired in the epididymal secretory compartment. It is not simply in equilibrium with a soluble pool on sperm — it is deposited onto the cell surface during transit through a specialized secretory niche and remains surface-accessible on sperm, mediating sperm-egg fusion. This fits the stable_surface_attachment contextual category.
